title: Semantic Pattern grammar sheet (v0)
description: Machine-readable declaration of a brand's visual pattern grammar. Slots mirror spec v0.1 section 3. Purely structural constraints live here; semantic checks that JSON Schema cannot express (canonical-peak rule, weight sums, operand references, relative-units sweep) are enforced by sheets.validate_sheet.

spec_version.description: Version of the meta-grammar spec this sheet is authored against.

status.description: audit-reconstruction = method demonstration on someone else's identity (spec 6.2: only the owner may enrol); enrolled = the owner has committed, signed and published.

issuer.description: Brand domain that issued the sheet. Nullable until enrolment mechanics exist (see SI-004).

identity_endpoint.description: Pointer to the signed semantic identity endpoint. Nullable until enrolment mechanics exist.

substrate.description: The substrate assumption colour/contrast measurements are relative to (spec 3.1).

structure.description: Organising system (spec 3.2). Governing dimensions in RELATIVE units only; any _px/_mm/_pt key is rejected by validate_sheet.

primitive_alphabet.description: Closed set of forms (spec 3.3). Dimensions module-relative or topology-relative only.

combination_rules.description: How primitives connect, cluster, repeat, offset and breathe (spec 3.4). Named values here may be referenced as operands of relational locus features.

layering.description: The layering/blend rule, e.g. multiply overprint (spec 3.5).

relationships.description: Committed orderings/ratios/consistencies that survive reproduction where absolute values do not (spec 3.5).

invariant.description: What remains fixed across the family (spec 3.6).

signature_locus.description: Where this identity's identification load lives (spec 3.7). REQUIRED.

measure.description: Name of a measurement procedure. A measure starting 'relation_' is relational and requires operands (enforced by schema below and by validate_sheet).

expected.description: Expected value: a number, an array (e.g. two hexes), a string, or null when unmeasured.

tolerance.description: Scalar tolerance, or an object such as {delta_e: 10} for colour measures. May be null for unmeasured features.

sample_unit.description: What 'n' counts for confidence scaling, e.g. band_boundaries, periods, inks_observed.

canonical_peaks.description: Crowded values near this feature. An identification feature whose expected sits within tolerance of any peak is rejected by validate_sheet (spec 3.7).

operands.description: For relational measures: ids referenced from combination_rules or other locus features.

status.description: unmeasured = weight reserved, value pending measurement; recogniser skips and renormalises.

allOf.description: role=identification requires positive weight and a sample_unit.
